steps.title: Compare the critiques
steps.content: Text 1 says modernist scholarship overrepresents experimental fragmentation and gives too little attention to coherent, linear works. Text 2 says scholars have focused too long on the fragmentation-versus-coherence contrast, which prevents them from noticing other stylistic similarities across modernist texts.

steps.title: Name the shared concern
steps.content: Both authors think the current focus makes scholarship's picture of modernist fiction too limited unless that focus changes. A is wrong because both texts still discuss style rather than calling for work beyond style. C invents reputation repair as the goal. D overstates the role of computer technology and says methods are becoming obsolete, which neither text claims. The answer is B.
